sections.heading: Introduction
sections.body:
Welcome to the documentation site for the official MongoDB Node.js driver.
You can add the driver to your application to work with MongoDB
in JavaScript or TypeScript. For more information about downloading and
installing the Node.js driver, see
[Download and Install](node-get-started-download-and-install) in the
Get Started guide.

You can connect using the Node.js driver for
deployments hosted in the following environments:

- [MongoDB Atlas](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/atlas): The fully
  managed service for MongoDB deployments in the cloud

- [MongoDB Enterprise](manual/administration/install-enterprise): The
  subscription-based, self-managed version of MongoDB

- [MongoDB Community](manual/administration/install-community): The
  source-available, free-to-use, and self-managed version of MongoDB

sections.heading: Get Started
sections.body:
Learn how to establish a connection to MongoDB Atlas and begin
working with data in the step-by-step Get Started tutorial.

sections.heading: Connect to MongoDB
sections.body:
Learn how to create and configure a connection to a MongoDB deployment in the
[Connect to MongoDB](node-connect) section.

sections.heading: Databases and Collections
sections.body:
Learn how to interact wth MongoDB databases and collections in the
[Databases and Collections](node-databases-collections) section.

sections.heading: Read and Write Data
sections.body: Learn how to find, update, and delete data in the [CRUD](node-insert) section.

sections.heading: Transform Your Data with Aggregation
sections.body:
Learn how to use the Node.js driver to perform aggregation operations in the
[Aggregation Operations](node-aggregation) section.

sections.heading: Data Formats
sections.body:
Learn how to work with BSON and other data formats in the
[Data Formats](node-bson) section.

sections.heading: Optimize Queries with Indexes
sections.body: Learn how to work with common types of indexes in the [Indexes for Query Optimization](node-indexes) section.

sections.heading: Run a Database Command
sections.body: Learn how to run a database command in the [Run a Database Command](node-run-command) section.

sections.heading: MongoDB Search
sections.body: Learn how to run MongoDB Search queries in the [Run a MongoDB Search Query](node-atlas-search) section.

sections.heading: MongoDB Vector Search
sections.body: Learn how to run MongoDB Vector Search queries in the [Run a MongoDB Vector Search Query](node-atlas-vector-search) section.

sections.heading: Monitoring and Logging
sections.body:
Learn how to monitor changes to your application and write them to logs in the
[Monitoring](node-monitoring) section.

sections.heading: Secure Your Data
sections.body:
Learn about ways you can authenticate your application and encrypt your data in

sections.heading: Reference
sections.body:
Find more information about Node.js driver versions, compatibility, and third-party tools in the
[Reference](node-release-notes) section.

sections.heading: API Documentation
sections.body:
For detailed information about classes and methods in the MongoDB
Node.js driver, see the [MongoDB Node.js driver API documentation](https://mongodb.github.io/node-mongodb-native/7.1).

sections.heading: Issues & Help
sections.body:
Learn how to report bugs, contribute to the driver, and to find help in the
Issues & Help section.

sections.heading: Object Document Mappers
sections.body:
MongoDB and our partners provide several object-document mappers (ODMs) for Node.js that
let developers work with MongoDB data as objects. One popular ODM is **Mongoose**,
which helps enforce a semi-rigid schema at the application level and provides features
to assist with data modeling and manipulation. **Prisma**, another ODM, helps
ensure data consistency by offering a type-safe database client and an intuitive schema.

For more information about using ODMs with MongoDB, see the following resources:

- [MongoDB ORMs, ODMs, and Libraries](https://www.mongodb.com/developer/products/mongodb/mongodb-orms-odms-libraries/?tck=docs)

- [Mongoose](https://mongoosejs.com/docs/guide.html) official documentation

- [Mongoose Get Started](node-mongoose-get-started) tutorial

- [Prisma](https://www.prisma.io/docs) official documentation

sections.heading: Packages
sections.body:
You can install the following packages to expand the functionality of the Node.js driver:

| Package                   | Description                                                                                |
| ------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| bson                      | BSON serialization and deserialization library used by the official MongoDB Node.js driver |
| kerberos                  | C++ extension for Node.js that provides support for Kerberos authentication                |
| mongodb-client-encryption | Library for client-side encryption functionality                                           |
| @mongodb-js/zstd          | Zstandard compression library for Node.js                                                  |

For information about each package's version compatibility, see the [Component Support Matrix](https://github.com//mongodb/node-mongodb-native#component-support-matrix) in the Node.js driver Github
repository.

sections.heading: Learn
sections.body:
To ask questions and engage in discussions with fellow developers who
use the Node.js driver, see the MongoDB communities on
[Reddit](https://www.reddit.com/r/mongodb/) or
[Stack Overflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/mongodb+node.js).

sections.heading: MongoDB University
sections.body: MongoDB University provides free courses to teach everyone how to use MongoDB.
